title: Explaining Symptoms
learningGoal: The learner will verbally construct sentences to accurately describe physical symptoms to a medical professional.
instructions: In this roleplay, a doctor asks what is wrong. Reply verbally in Japanese based on the English prompts, stating your symptoms clearly.

contentItems.text: Speak clearly. Remember to use 'ga' with body parts and pain.

speakingPrompt: Translate: 1) My stomach hurts. 2) I have a fever. 3) Please give me medicine.

subTasks.prompt: State your pain. Say: 'My stomach hurts.'

sampleResponse.targetText: おなかが いたいです。

subTasks.prompt: State your other symptom. Say: 'I have a fever.'
Request treatment. Say: 'Please give me medicine.'

sampleResponse.targetText: くすりを ください。

baselineTranscriptSentences.targetText: おなかが いたいです。
くすりを ください。

acceptableParaphrases: Accept 'Watashi wa onaka ga itai desu'.

criteria.name: Symptom Accuracy 1
criteria.description: Did the learner say 'Onaka ga itai' correctly?

criteria.name: Symptom Accuracy 2
criteria.description: Did the learner say 'Netsu ga arimasu' correctly?

criteria.name: Request Accuracy
criteria.description: Did the learner correctly ask for 'Kusuri' (medicine)?

adminCorrectAnswerSet.sampleAnswer: おなかが いたいです。ねつが あります。くすりを ください。

feedbackRules.onPass: Fantastic speaking! You conveyed your symptoms perfectly.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the medical vocabulary. 'Onaka' means stomach. 'Atama' means head.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good try! Make sure you ask for medicine ('kusuri') at the end.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: どうしましたか。おなかが いたいです。ねつが あります。くすりを ください。

hintText: Use 'ga' to mark what is hurting or what symptom you have.
